assumptions: fct_trading_daily already filters to completed transactions per the canonical contract.

derivation_trace.aggregator_rationale: SUM because gtv_idr is per-(transaction_date, asset_class) row, summable across days.
derivation_trace.rendered_why_chosen: Picked fct_trading_daily because grain (transaction_date, asset_class) matches required (transaction_date, asset_class), and every scope predicate (October 2025) is feasible on it. Rejected: no alternatives considered. Filters: transaction_date >= '2025-10-01'; transaction_date < '2025-11-01'. Aggregator: SUM (SUM because gtv_idr is per-(transaction_date, asset_class) row, summable across days.).

logic: Aggregate gtv_idr by asset_class for the October 2025 window.
question: How did GTV stack up across asset classes for October 2025?

source.why_chosen: Picked fct_trading_daily because grain (transaction_date, asset_class) matches required (transaction_date, asset_class), and every scope predicate (October 2025) is feasible on it. Rejected: no alternatives considered. Filters: transaction_date >= '2025-10-01'; transaction_date < '2025-11-01'. Aggregator: SUM (SUM because gtv_idr is per-(transaction_date, asset_class) row, summable across days.).

sql: SELECT asset_class, SUM(gtv_idr) AS gtv_idr FROM fct_trading_daily WHERE transaction_date >= '2025-10-01' AND transaction_date < '2025-11-01' GROUP BY asset_class ORDER BY gtv_idr DESC
